mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate, when it is already above the zero bound, signals easing financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equity cash flows. That should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month because valuation expansion dominates any minor growth slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS falls for two consecutive monthly observations, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains materially below CPI inflation, real policy rates stay deeply negative and liquidity conditions remain supportive for equities. This should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through a reflation and duration-multiple channel.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate negative 5-day forward returns more often than positive ones, or if the trigger no longer predicts higher returns after the inflation gap narrows below 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and a higher probability of earnings downgrades, credit stress, and recession pricing. That should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days because investors reprice cyclical profits before macro data fully deteriorates.
failure_condition: Reject if rising UNRATE fails to precede below-zero 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the 20-day forward return distribution is indistinguishable from unconditional returns in periods when UNRATE rises over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a genuine employment deterioration rather than a noisy labor-market adjustment. That combination should be bearish for equities over the next 60 trading days because it confirms falling income growth and weaker demand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 2 months while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger does not lead to negative 60-day forward returns, or if either single series alone explains the effect without loss of predictive power from the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer and retail demand, which supports revenue growth expectations and operating leverage for index constituents. That should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as analysts revise earnings upward.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not coincide with positive 20-day forward returns, or if the signal loses power when retail sales growth is above its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO together with weak HOUST captures a broad supply-side slowdown in production and construction. That should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because it signals margin pressure, weaker capex, and slower earnings growth.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and HOUST does not precede negative 60-day returns, or if the joint signal adds no information beyond INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after a contraction signals that aggregate demand is reaccelerating from a depressed base. In that regime, equities should post positive returns over the next 60 trading days as recession discounting fades and cyclicals recover.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips during confirmed post-recession expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating and FEDFUNDS is rising at the same time, inflation pressure and policy tightening jointly compress equity multiples. That combination should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because the market prices a more restrictive real-rate path.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises while FEDFUNDS increases over the prior 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-tightening trigger does not precede negative 20-day returns, or if returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.
